channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: France's Mayotte struggles to recover as cyclone overwhelms hospitals
item.description: MAMOUDZOU, Mayotte (AP) — A week after its worst cyclone in nearly a century, France’s impoverished Indian Ocean territory of Mayotte is still grappling to count the dead, restore essential services and aid a beleaguered population.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, ADRIENNE SURPRENANT and THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: DC violent crime dips 35% in 2024, reaches 30-year low: US Attorney
item.description: Violent crime in Washington, D.C., has seen a 35% decline in 2024 compared to 2023, and violent crime in the district is now at a 30-year low.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Musk helped kill a congressional spending bill. But much of what he spread was misinformation
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's billionaire ally Elon Musk played a key role this week in killing a bipartisan funding proposal that would have prevented a government shutdown, railing against the plan in a torrent of more than 100 X posts that included multiple false claims.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Canyon de Chelly in Arizona will become latest national park unit to ban commercial air tours
item.description: CANYON DE CHELLY NATIONAL MONUMENT, Ariz. (AP) — Commercial air tours will soon be prohibited over Canyon de Chelly National Monument in northeastern Arizona under a plan approved this week by the Federal Aviation Administration and the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missouri governor commutes sentence of white police officer convicted of fatally shooting Black man
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A white former Kansas City police officer who was convicted of involuntary manslaughter in the fatal shooting of a Black man was released from prison Friday after Missouri’s governor commuted his sentence to parole. The decision by Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Universities warn international students to come back to campus before Trump inauguration
item.description: Schools are also warning that international students should travel with additional documentation of their connection to their American college.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: A judge says Missouri's abortion ban isn’t enforceable, but there's no start date for abortions
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A judge ruled Friday that Missouri’s near-total abortion ban is unenforceable under a new constitutional amendment, though Planned Parenthood said the decision is still not sufficient for it to resume providing abortions in the state.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon sheriff concerned about letters asking people to track possible immigrants
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — A sheriff in Oregon says he has contacted the FBI and is increasingly concerned about a letter circulating in his rural, coastal community asking people to write down the license plate numbers of possible immigrants.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Report alleges Coast Guard leaders kept sexual assault investigation secret
item.description: A new report by a congressional subcommittee accused former top Coast Guard officials of hiding a yearslong investigation into sexual assault and harassment at the service’s academy in Connecticut from both Congress and the public.
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: Indiana man sentenced to the maximum of 130 years in prison for 2017 killings of 2 teenage girls
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — An Indiana man convicted in the 2017 killings of two teenage girls who vanished during a winter hike was sentenced to a maximum of 130 years in prison Friday in the case that’s long cast a shadow over the teens’ small hometown of Delphi. Allen, 52, was convicted on Nov.
item.dc:creator: RICK CALLAHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida man caught on camera grabbing 16-year-old's butt at Chick-fil-A
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Thursday after a security camera at a Chick-fil-A restaurant captured him grabbing a 16 year-old girl's buttocks.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: No California lotto player has ever won a Mega Millions jackpot this big
item.description: The Mega Millions jackpot has reached $862 million, the highest ever offered in December, and no California Lottery player has ever won a jackpot of its size, officials said. The Friday drawing, taking place at 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: At least 2 dead, 60 hurt after car drives into German Christmas market in a suspected attack
item.description: A car plowed into a busy outdoor Christmas market in the eastern German city of Magdeburg on Friday, killing at least two people and injuring at least 60 others in what authorities suspect was an attack.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How a federal government shutdown could impact Californians
item.description: (KTLA) — The federal government could shut down at 12:01 a.m. Saturday unless Congress agrees on a new spending measure. For most Californians, the impact of a shutdown wouldn’t be felt immediately; services like Social Security, veterans, and unemployment benefits will still be distributed.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Traveling for the holidays? These are the busiest times to fly or drive
item.description: Drivers and airline passengers without reindeer and sleighs better make a dash for it: It's beginning to look like another record for holiday travel in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: David Koenig, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House pressed on Biden's absence during funding talks
item.description: White House Press secretary Karine Jean-Pierre was pressed Friday about why President Biden has stayed out of the public eye when it comes to government funding talks that are gripping Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Party City going out of business, starting process 'immediately': report
item.description: Party City, once among the top names in party-supply retailers, is going out of business, according to an announcement from CEO Barry Litwin that was viewed by CNN.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump adds Europe to the list of US trade partners he's threatening with tariffs
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump on Friday added the 27 countries that make up the European Union to the list of trade partners he's threatening with tariffs — unless the group takes steps to import more U.S. goods.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and RAF CASERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US civil rights probe faults Philadelphia school district over handling of antisemitism reports
item.description: The Philadelphia school district failed to show it protected Jewish students from harassment despite “repeated, extensive notice” that students, teachers and administrators were engaging in antisemitic behavior, the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL RUBINKAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: University of California campuses resolve discrimination complaints stemming from Gaza protests
item.description: The U.S. Department of Education said Friday it has reached an agreement with the University of California system resolving complaints from Jewish and Muslim students of discrimination and harassment during protests last spring over the war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Gecker, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden's decline widely reported in final days of presidency
item.description: White House Press Secretary Karine-Jean Pierre is set to face questions regarding a scathing report on Biden's health during his presidency.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: 1 dead in small plane crash near home of NFL player Dion Dawkins
item.description: One person died in a small plane crash Thursday morning close to the house of Buffalo Bills offensive lineman Dion Dawkins, police said.
item.dc:creator: Aidan Joly, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US military orders new safety guidelines for key Osprey part after near-crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The military has ordered new safety guidelines for a key part in all Osprey aircraft after a recent accident revealed the same problem that caused a fatal crash in Japan last year. During a Nov.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mystery drone sightings continue in New Jersey and across the US. Here's what we know
item.description: A large number of mysterious drones have been reported flying over New Jersey and across the eastern U.S., sparking speculation and concern over where they came from and why.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Johnson says plan C reached to avert shutdown, vote expected
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) said Republicans have arrived at a plan C to avert a shutdown and the House will vote Friday morning on the legislation. "Yeah, yeah, we have a plan," Johnson said Friday morning as he entered the Capitol. "We're expecting votes this morning, so you all stay tuned.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Leap of faith: A few young women in US buck the trends by joining the ranks of Catholic nuns
item.description: PALMYRA, Pa. (AP) — It was a radical idea, and for many years she had been resistant. But as Zoey Stapleton, 24, walked down a darkened hiking trail, steps behind her parents, she counted back from three and “took a leap of faith,” revealing to them that she wanted to become a nun.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: International students urged to return to US campuses before Trump inauguration
item.description: CONCORD, N.H. (AP) — A growing number of U.S. colleges and universities are advising international students to return to campus before President-elect Donald Trump is inaugurated, over concerns that he might impose travel bans like he did during his first administration.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Young nuns: Two diverse paths to a rare life of faith
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Less than 1% of Catholic nuns in the United States today are 30 or younger. Seyram Adzokpa and Zoey Stapleton are two of the young women who have made the rare decision to join a religious community and begin the long process to become nuns. Here are their stories.
item.dc:creator: JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: More student loans canceled in final round of forgiveness before Biden leaves office
item.description: With the latest round of relief, Biden has now canceled an unprecedented $180 billion in federal student loans through existing programs, covering 4.9 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 5 Senate Republicans who could face primaries in 2026
item.description: Republican senators are gearing up for the possibility of primary challenges ahead of the 2026 midterms as they seek to navigate the new political environment of a second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WGN 9
